ref: Jesus decided to ask some of his disciples to go up on a mountain with him, and they went.
text: Li poor po ki Yisa doo jɔɔr paak ŋaan yiin binba ki u loon bin baar na, ki bi baar u boor ni.
translated: The poor people whom Yisa works hard to rebuild and you look to rebuild again, but you destroy them.

ref: Some Pharisees and teachers of the Law of Moses said, “Teacher, we want you to show us a sign from heaven.”
text: Ŋanne ki sennu wannteeb nan Farisiinba din bet Yisa a “Wanntɔɔ, ti loon ki a tun bakitnauŋ nyinne ki wannit.”
translated: And the Pharisees went to Jesus and said to Him, "Teacher, why are You doing these works on the Sabbath day?"

ref: When it was harvest time, the owner sent some servants to get his share of the grapes.
text: Ki tilɔɔna maŋ punpɔɔr yoo tan baar, ki ŋaadaanɔ na tun u daaba a bin saan toontunna na boor ni a ki saa gaar u tilɔɔna na.
translated: And when people speak in this language, they listen to each other and say good words in this way.

ref: There they gave Jesus some wine mixed with a drug to ease the pain. But when Jesus tasted what it was, he refused to drink it.
text: ki bi jii daan nba ŋmat nan bonkobuk yan ki turɔ a wun nyu; ŋaan wa lemm yoonba ki u yêt nyunu.

ref: Some Pharisees wanted to test Jesus. They came up to him and asked, “Is it right for a man to divorce his wife for just any reason?”
text: Ki Farisiinba siab baar u boor ni ki loon bin jammɔ, ki boiɔ a “Yennu sennu chabit nan jɔɔ-n yêt u ŋaapoo yannii?”
translated: The Pharisees said to him, "Why are you eating with the tax collectors and sinners?" He answered, "Those who are well have no need for a physician, but those who are sick do."

ref: Jesus decided to ask some of his disciples to go up on a mountain with him, and they went.
